r i 1'r 1'

* [...]

(If) [...].

r i 2'2'

* x [...]

(If) ... [...].

r i 3'3'

* ZA₃ [...]

(If) on the right [...].

r i 4'4'

* 1/2 ⸢GAL₂⸣ [...]

(If) there is 1/2 [...].

r i 5'5'

* ana 2-šu₂ ⸢tak⸣-[ši-tu₄? ...]

(If) twice: profit [...]

r i 6'6'

* ana 3-šu₂ tak-⸢ši⸣-[tu₄ ...]

(If) three times: profit [...].

r i 7'7'

* ZA₃ ṣa!(NIG₂-)pi-⸢ir⸣ [...]

(If) it is ingrown on the right [...].

r i 8'8'

* ka-pil [...]

(If) it is rolled up [...].

r i 9'9'

* ma-[...]

(If) ... [...].

r i 10'10'

* uḫ-ḫu-ur i-šar₂-ru₃ [...]

(If) it is remaining: he will become rich [...].

r i 11'11'

* ub-bu-uḫ!(ḪU) i-šar₂-ru₃ [...]

(If) it is surrounded: he will become rich [...].

r i 12'12'

* sa-niq ŠU.BI.AŠ.AM₃<(i-šar₂-ru₃)> [...]

(If) it is stretched: ditto (he will become rich) [...].

r i 13'13'

* pe-ti ŠU.BI.AŠ.AM₃<(i-šar₂-ru₃)> [...]

(If) it is open: ditto (he will become rich) [...].

r i 14'14'

* ME.ZE₂-BI GID₂.DA-MEŠ la-la-ni [...]

(If) his jaws are long: flourishing of [...].

r i 15'15'

* LUGUD₂.DA-MEŠ ŠA₃.ḪUL IGI : UŠ₂ [...]

(If) they are short: he will see sorrow (or): he will die. [...].

r i 16'16'

* ana mi-na-a!-ti-šu₂ GID₂.DA-⸢MEŠ⸣ [...]

(If) they are long in proportion to (the rest of) him: [...].

r i 17'17'

* la-ḫu-šu₂ paṭ-ra mim-ma la šu-[a-tu₂ ...]

(If) his jaws are loose: something that is not his [...].

r i 18'18'

* la-ḫu-šu₂ paṭ-ra u₃ IGI-MIN-šu₂ ⸢SIG₇⸣ [...]

(If) his jaws are loose and his eyes are yellow-green: [...].

r i 19'19'

* lu-ʾ-a-a-šu₂ ba-aʾ-la NA BI sa-a-ar x [...]

(If) his tonsils are large: that man is a criminal [...].

r i 20'20'

* raq-qa NA BI ŠA₃.ḪUL [...]

(If) they are thin: that man [...] sorrow.

r i 21'21'

* pa-al-ša : pa-al-ša₂ im-ṭu₂-u₂ GAR⸢nu⸣-[šu₂ ...]

(If) they are pierced (or variant spelling): pierced: losses will be inflicted on him [...].

r i 22'22'

* TUR-MEŠ al-ma-nu-⸢tu₂⸣ [...]

(If) they are small: widowerhood [...].

r i 23'23'

* TE-MEŠ-šu₂ tur-ru-pa!(MA) : TUR-MEŠ al-ma-nu-tu₂ ⸢DU⸣[ak]

(If) his cheeks are discoloured (or): small: he will become a widower.

r i 24'24'

* ḫab-ša₂ ina KI KAR <sun>-gi-ir-tu₂ ma-⸢aʾ⸣-[da]

(If) they are crushed: in places of desolation there will be many sungīrtu-plants.

r i 25'25'

* um-ṣa-ti SA₅-MEŠ ⸢IGI⸣ [...]

(If) they are covered in moles: ... [...].

r i 26'26'

* TE-su ša₂ ZA₃ pe-en₆-de!-e ma-li-a-at ek-⸢liš⸣ [...]

(If) his right cheek is covered in pendû-marks: [...] gloomily.

r i 27'27'

* ša GUB₃ MIN<(ša₂)> ZA₃ pe-en₆-de!-e ma-li-a-at nam-riš DU-[MEŠ]

(If) his left cheek is covered in pendû-marks: he will always go radiantly.

r i 28'28'

* bi-nu-ut TE i-šu! E₂ GURUŠ-MEŠ DU₃[uš]

(If) he has a growth on his cheek: he will build a house (full) of young men.

r i 29'29'

* la i-šu sa-ar₂-tu₂ TUKUši : u₂ NIG₂.TUK [...]

(If) he does not have (one): he will acquire falsehood (or): and [...] riches.

r i 30'30'

* TUKU ina E₂ KI.TUŠ A₂.TUKU [...]

(If) he has (one): in the house of (his) dwelling [...] profit.

r i 31'31'

* la₃ TUKU ina E₂ KI.TUŠ gi-in-na-tu₂ TUKU[ši]

(If) he does not have (one): he will become trapped in the house of (his) dwelling.

r i 32'32'

* SIG₂ TE e-bi ŠA₃-BI NU DU₁₀.GA [...]

(If) the hair on his cheek is thick: he will not be satisfied; [...].

r i 33'33'

* ra-aq ŠA₃-BI ⸢DU₁₀⸣.[GA ...]

(If) it is thin: he will be satisfied; [...].

r i 34'34'

* i-šu sa-ma-ru GAR [...]

(If) he has (hair and) it is curly: [...].

r i 35'35'

* ap-par-ri-i-iʾ i-la-ni al-ma-nu-tu₂ DU : ina [...]

(If) it is tufted hair: he is favoured by the gods; he will become a widow (or): in [...].

r i 36'36'

* SIG₂ TE ṣap-rat NIG₂.TUK [...]

(If) the hair on his cheek is ingrown: [...] riches.

r i 37'37'

* ša-liṭ : sa-lit ŠA₃ TI.LA TUKU [...]

(If) it is abundant (or): split: he will have good health; [...].

r i 38'38'

* ma-sik : x x ⸢U₄⸣-MEŠ-šu₂ LUGUD₂.DA-MEŠ : sa-da-ru GAR-[šu₂]

(If) it is ugly (or): ...: his day will be short (or): doing (things) regularly will be provided [for him].

r i 39'39'

* ma-sik-ma x [...]-šu₂ pa-as-lat KI.MIN pa-aš-ṭa-at NA BI INIM.GAR-[šu₂ ...]

(If) it is ugly and his [...] is twisted; alternatively, obliterated: that man [...] his utterance.

r i 40'40'

* SA₅ NA ⸢BI⸣ [...] A₂ U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ GID₂.DA-[MEŠ]

(If) it is covered: that man [...]; his days will be long.

r i 41'41'

* ka-bar A [...] NIG₂ [...]

(If) it is thick: ... [...] .... [...].

r i 42'42'

* qa₂-⸢ta⸣-[an ...] x BAD U₄ x [...]

(If) it is very thin: [...] ... [...].

r i 43'43'

* x [...] a-na TI.[LA?-šu₂? ...]

(If) ... [...] for his life [...].

r i 44'44'

⸢*⸣ [...] x [...]

(If) [...] ... [...].

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
(start of column missing)
r ii 1'r 1'

[... UKU₂]in

[...] will become poor.

r ii 2'2'

[...] GAR-šu₂

[...] will be provided with [...].

r ii 3'3'

[...] GA

[...] ....

r ii 4'4'

[...] SU

[...] ....

r ii 5'5'

[...] UŠ

[...] ....

r ii 6'6'

[...] GAZ

[...] will be killed.

r ii 7'7'

[... UKU₂]in

[...] will become poor.

r ii 8'8'

[...] x IGI

[...] will see [...].

r ii 9'9'

[...] x E₂ NUNtu₂ DU₃uš

[...] ... he will build a house of rulership.

r ii 10'10'

[...]-te-di GAL₂-ma UŠ₂ ŠA₃.GAR UŠ₂

[...] there will be ...; the dying will die of hunger.

r ii 11'11'

[...]-x-su TIqe₂ : ma-aʾ-du NU GU₇

[...] he will take his [...] (or): he will not eat much.

r ii 12'12'

[...]-MEŠ : zik-ri ŠUB-MEŠ

[...] ... (or): (his) reputation will constantly fall.

r ii 13'13'

[...] x šu₂-a-tu₄ ŠU-su KURad₂

[...] he (lit. his hand) will conquer that ....

r ii 14'14'

[...] A ina gišTUKUL GAZ

[...] ... he will be killed with a weapon.

r ii 15'15'

[...] šar-ri ina-ziq U₄-MEŠ-šu₂ i-ṣu

[...] of the king will worry; his days will be few.

r ii 16'16'

[...] ⸢alan⸣-dim₂-mu-u₂ la₃ AL.TIL up-puš₄

[...] Alandimmû, not completed; properly executed.

(catchline)
r ii 17'17'

[... ba-aʾ]-la DINGIRni ina gišTUKUL ŠUBut KI.MIN mu-še-ziq

[...] are large: he is favoured by the gods; he will die by a weapon; alternatively, he is a troublemaker.

(colophon)
r ii 18'18'

[... m]⸢dINANA⸣—MU—KAM ŠA₃.BAL.BAL mE₂.KUR—za-kir lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ

[...] Ištar-šum-ereš, descendant of Ekur-zakir, incantation priest.

r ii 19'19'

[...] ⸢DUMU⸣ md60—ŠEŠ-MEŠ—MU A mŠU—60

[...], son of Anu-ahhe-iddin, descendant of Gimil-Anu.

(rest of column blank)
